name: Hot Spots (Acute Moist Dermatitis)
description: Hot spots are rapidly developing areas of acute moist skin inflammation caused by self-trauma from scratching, licking, or chewing. They are secondary to an underlying trigger such as allergies, insect bites, or poor coat condition.
treatment_overview: Clip and clean the area, apply topical antiseptic, and treat with topical or systemic antibiotics and steroids. An E-collar prevents further self-trauma. Identify and treat the underlying trigger.
prevention_tips: Keep coat clean and dry in humid weather
